title: The mysteries surrounding Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: The mysteries surrounding Evangelion             Quote Post by pen.pen » Mon Jan 06, 2014 5:13 am Hi everyone, first of all I am not quite sure if this post is in the right section. It is my first thread in the forum. Ever since I finished NGE, both endings, I tried to find all kind of explanations related to the plot itself, as many of you. I happened to find this interesting forum and then had a glance at a few articles from the wiki (which is amazing!). I soon realised there was not an "answer" to all of that but, instead, many theories concerning different aspects of the show. That surprised me a lot, and I knew I was facing a different Anime, unlike any other I've ever watched. Apparently, there are lots of "clues" behind every frame, hiding part of the "truth" (if there is any). I find this amazing because you have to search, investigate and theorize to discover some hidden paths of the show. But...when do we stop? Well, getting to the point: I was wondering if there really is so much effort put into "hiding" things like explanations, motivations,etc. Is Evangelion just a bunch of ideas tied together?. I've been reading some Anno's interviews and statements. He goes like "yes, we put that because it was funny". Things like that. Then, I think of an example. The "ghostly appearances of Rei", like the one in the first chapter. Maybe, there is no "logical" explanation related to the plot itself. Instead, it might be a resource from the director to create mystery around that character from the beginning or maybe ,just to "play around". But again, that would be nothing but a theory. Yes, he states that we should find our own answers. However, I wonder how many scenes like that could be there just to impact on imagery and add turbulent ambient (Why did Shinji choke Asuka!?).The same could be applied to the dialogs. I don't know if the point is clear enough. Perhaps you know more about him or understand the show better ( I am sure of this), because now after reading a little, I am completely lost (and so tired that I doubt this post will turn into any kind of debate). Thanks for reading! (I am sorry if my english is not clear enough )

[]: Re: The mysteries surrounding Evangelion             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Mon Jan 06, 2014 7:01 am To use some exaggerated terms: Not even science gives more than theories. ;) (See my signature) "Lengthy" explanation: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Jan 06, 2014 8:49 am You dance with the devil, the devil don't change, man. The devil changes you.

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Mon Jan 06, 2014 11:40 pm Gorbatschow , thanks for the answer!. It 'll take me some time to read all of that, but looks really interesting. I guess NGE is a puzzle after all.... Oh,and yes, it would be nice to read your opinion about that. Chuckman , I am not quite sure I understood what you said Reading about "Rei 's ghostly appearances", also watched that video, a few questions arised. The idea of "Giant Rei" being able to send instances to the past and future might explain why she appeared before Misato and Ritsuko in their last moments of life. But, assuming this correct, why would she spawn in the middle of Tokyo-3( first episode).? Or, what is more, why would she appear in the future(the last scene)? What soul did she have to collect?. I don't find any "logical" explanation, though, I might be losing something.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Wed Jan 08, 2014 10:50 am ^ I'll write it around the weekend, since I'm kind of busy right now, furthermore I'll send it via PN, because it would be a bit offtopic. So, stay tuned

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed Jan 08, 2014 10:54 am pen.pen wrote: Reading about "Rei 's ghostly appearances", also watched that video, a few questions arised. The idea of "Giant Rei" being able to send instances to the past and future might explain why she appeared before Misato and Ritsuko in their last moments of life. But, assuming this correct, why would she spawn in the middle of Tokyo-3( first episode).? Or, what is more, why would she appear in the future(the last scene)? What soul did she have to collect?. I don't find any "logical" explanation, though, I might be losing something.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Wed Jan 08, 2014 11:14 am Shinoyami65 wrote: Well, from a theatrical viewpoint it's a sort of bookend for the series- Rei appears when the series begins and she appears when it finally ends. In-universe, perhaps Rei/Lilith was observing the beginning and the end of Shinji's journey through the events of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Wed Jan 08, 2014 5:11 pm Well, that makes sense so, I agree with you both. It might be a film resource to link both, the beginning and ending. This, among other things, is , from my point of view, what makes Evangelion a remarkable artwork. Little details that cover the show with a mysterious ambience. As for the second argument, in-Universe , you provide, it fits the plot really well but, like everything in the NGE world, might allow different interpretations. By the way, I found this thread that discuss pretty much of the aspects concerning Rei' s appearances. http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14771/Questions-about-Rei-in-Episode-1/. Thanks again , I am willing to read tour opinión , Gorba. Take your time Cheers

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 08, 2014 7:05 pm Saw that the video was by V; closed the window immediately. Trigger warning would have been nice. ;p

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 08, 2014 7:12 pm Someone in the comments called it "eva as explained by Alan Alda."

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Wed Jan 08, 2014 8:22 pm What is "wrong" whit that V guy?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 09, 2014 2:05 am pen.pen wrote: What is "wrong" whit that V guy?

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Thu Jan 09, 2014 8:41 am Mr. Tines wrote: An excess of enthusiasm coupled with a tendency to be dogmatic, mainly.

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Tue Jan 28, 2014 10:31 pm Hello there again!. I was thinking about a scene ,from chapter chapter 18, the other day, and wonder if anyone noticed This ( I Couldn't embed the image :/). Then, in contrast to what is seen there, a few chapters later, we are shown Gendo doing nothing to prevent Armisael to "achieve first level contact with Unit 00" while invading it. I tought it was curious the way he acted. First, I can't think of a convincing idea that explains why he bypassed that "neuronal connection", thus causing Rei so much pain. Maybe to gain some time?.Maybe. It seems he wanted to prevent the infection no matter what, even if it would hurt her. We all know what he is capable of to achieve his goals and, he had to keep Rei alive, even if she can be replaced?, until the very end. From this point of view, it looks as if he treated her as a kind of "instrument", doesn't it? On the other hand, we have seen him ,in an earlier episode, opening the entry plug with his own bare hands, something ,I would say, "unusual" in a man like Gendo Ikari. Finally, episode 23. He didn´t show any intention to avoid Eva 00, and Rei, becoming infected. Maybe it spread so fast he couldn't react?. Maybe. Also, he still needed Rei. What really calls my attention is the different reactions to "similar" events. Perhaps, I am getting all wrong. I wanted to know what you guys think about this tiny detail :P (The underlined passage is because I think it is a weak idea)[/img]

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 29, 2014 6:52 am Armisael is entering via 00's torso rather than just a limb so I doubt there's really much that can be done about it. Thus he takes the extrordinary measure of sending Shinji and 01 out of lockdown to help, which he very specifically was NOT willing to do for Asuka's sake.

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Wed Jan 29, 2014 1:13 pm Uhm, you are right. That would explain Asuka 's words " Am I so worthless to you?. You didn't do this for me" after being sent back. I guess she is refering to what happened before, when her mind was raped. Ok so, after all, he really wanted to prevent Rei from dying, or maybe just Unit 00?, as he used his last resort to have her rescued. Still, I find it curious the fact exposed in the previous footage I showed you :/. Did he prioritized Eva 00 's integrity over Rei?.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 29, 2014 1:46 pm He only cares about 00 in so far as it's got a part of Rei/Lilith in it. Rei is his trump card against Seele, so naturally he values her very highly.

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Thu Jan 30, 2014 1:47 am Then, Gendo values Rei highly, but in which way? We are shown that she is the only person who Gendo appreciates. This might have to do with the origin of Rei. She takes after Yui, she is actually her clone, and has Lilith soul. A perfect "daughter" for Gendo?. Beside this, she is the key that would lead his plan to be carried out successfuly. That being said, I could think of two reason to justify the following facts/evidence: --1)As mentioned before, the episode where he rescued Rei from the entry plug, during the eva 00 activation test. --> He acts like that because he truly cares about her, as a person and as an "instrument". I cant provide further examples right now, to sustain this point. Assuming that correct, let se if that argument can verify --2) The footage I showed you previously. -->It seems he does not consider Rei `s integrity a "priority". Why did he bypassed Neuronal disconnection?. It can be argued that the process would take much time, really?, and so it would be risky , as the angel might take control of eva 00 and seriously damage Rei. Assuming this, his reaction would be "logical", as it was the safest way to proceed in such situation. Still, it can be concluded that the argument in 1) is somehow correct.( he appreciates her both ways) Another interpretation would be, leaving behind the time factor, that either he does not care about her as a person at all or he just prioritize Eva 00, which would imply that Gendo is arrogant to the point he considers his plan above all. Then, if the former is correct, argument in 1) would fall apart. It can be argued that Rei is replaceable so there is no need , for Gendo, to be concerned. However, this would contradict the footage evidence from 1) where he truly,for whatever the reason, is worried about Rei. It is safe,then, to conclude he JUST prioritize eva, his plan, and himself over Rei, to whom he shares some "weird" affection, ( I would say an affection "Gendo style") thus argument in 1) might still be correct, but the part "he appreciates her as a person" would be weaker.) It was fun to write this lol. It would be nice to know wheter you agree or disagree with it. Maybe you could add your own evidence or opinion :).

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jan 30, 2014 2:24 am When it comes to Gendo, his plans are ultimately what he cares most about, however that's not to say that he doesn't care about other things; he is a complex individual, just like the rest of the cast. When it is revealed in EoE his feeling about Shinji, a new picture is painted upon all his personal relationships in general: he keeps others at a distance while reserving his emotions, to keep himself from hurting others with his flaws while at the same time preventing himself from being touched by the emotions of others. However, no matter how aloof he tries to be, he does let his emotions out when caught off guard; we see this with Rei's activation test. He obviously values her as part of his grand plan, but even if she is just a piece of the puzzle that can ultimately be replaced, she is also a person he is shown to happily converse with from time to time earlier in the show. Gendo, even though he often portrays himself to be, isn't completely devoid of empathy. He clearly cares about Shinji, yet treats him like he doesn't exists, so he likely cares for Rei enough to avoid needlessly recreating her memories and such when he can. Like Ritsuko said, he's just not quite so adept at living. And believe me, Ritsuko would know. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Thu Jan 30, 2014 3:51 pm Sure. It seems like Gendo does suffer from "Hedgehog dilemma" , dont you think?. This "dilemma" is quite recurrent, and seems to apply to many characters. It might be a hole in the darkness of human souls. A hole that can be filled through instrumentality. What do you mean by so he likely cares for Rei enough to avoid needlessly recreating her memories and such when he can.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jan 30, 2014 2:24 am When it comes to Gendo, his plans are ultimately what he cares most about, however that's not to say that he doesn't care about other things; he is a complex individual, just like the rest of the cast. When it is revealed

Article: The mysteries surrounding Evangelion
 When it comes to Gendo, his plans are ultimately what he cares most about, however that's not to say that he doesn't care about other things; he is a complex individual, just like the rest of the cast. When it is revealed in EoE his feeling about Shinji, a new picture is painted upon all his personal relationships in general: he keeps others at a distance while reserving his emotions, to keep himself from hurting others with his flaws while at the same time preventing himself from being touched by the emotions of others. However, no matter how aloof he tries to be, he does let his emotions out when caught off guard; we see this with Rei's activation test. He obviously values her as part of his grand plan, but even if she is just a piece of the puzzle that can ultimately be replaced, she is also a person he is shown to happily converse with from time to time earlier in the show. Gendo, even though he often portrays himself to be, isn't completely devoid of empathy. He clearly cares about Shinji, yet treats him like he doesn't exists, so he likely cares for Rei enough to avoid needlessly recreating her memories and such when he can. Like Ritsuko said, he's just not quite so adept at living. And believe me, Ritsuko would know. ;)

What do you mean by so he likely cares for Rei enough to avoid needlessly recreating her memories and such when he can.
